explanation: Colloidal oatmeal is finely milled whole oat flour approved as an FDA OTC skin protectant active, clinically documented to reduce histamine-triggered itch, calm inflammation, and reinforce the skin barrier through its beta-glucan and avenanthramide content. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and among the most evidence-backed soothing ingredients available for reactive, eczema-prone, and sensitized skin.

Colloidal oatmeal (2%) water is a water phase incorporating 2% colloidal oatmeal — finely milled whole oat with FDA OTC skin protectant status — delivering beta-glucans and avenanthramides that calm itch and reinforce the barrier directly into the formula's aqueous base. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in products formulated specifically for reactive, eczema-prone, and sensitive skin.

Copper gluconate is the copper salt of gluconic acid, used in skincare as a source of bioavailable copper ions that activate superoxide dismutase (an endogenous antioxidant enzyme) and support wound healing and collagen cross-linking. It is well-tolerated at typical use concentrations and is used in firming and protective formulas for its ability to enhance the skin's own antioxidant defenses.

Copper gluconate is the copper salt of gluconic acid, providing bioavailable copper ions that activate superoxide dismutase and support collagen cross-linking and wound healing. It is well-tolerated and used in firming and protective formulas for its role in enhancing the skin's antioxidant enzyme activity.

Copper tripeptide-1 (GHK-Cu) is one of the most studied skin-active peptides — a naturally occurring copper complex that activates fibroblasts to produce collagen and glycosaminoglycans, accelerates wound healing, and has documented antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating, and is used in anti-aging and regenerative formulas for its broad, evidence-supported tissue-repair benefits.

Copper tripeptide-1 (GHK-Cu) is a naturally occurring copper-peptide complex that stimulates collagen and elastin synthesis, accelerates wound healing, and activates antioxidant defense pathways. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating, and is used in anti-aging and barrier-repairing formulas for its broad, clinically studied regenerative properties.

Coptis japonica (Japanese goldthread) root extract is a botanical antioxidant rich in berberine and related isoquinoline alkaloids with documented antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in clarifying and protective formulas for its ability to inhibit acne-causing bacteria and neutralize free radicals.

Corallina officinalis extract is derived from coralline red algae and provides a rich mineral profile alongside polysaccharides and phenolic compounds with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in marine-ingredient formulas for its ability to protect skin from oxidative stress and deliver trace minerals.

Cordyceps sinensis (caterpillar fungus) extract is an adaptogenic fungal ingredient rich in cordycepin, polysaccharides, and antioxidants used in skincare for its free-radical-scavenging and cellular energy-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its antioxidant and regenerative benefits.

Corylus avellana (hazelnut) seed oil is a lightweight plant oil rich in oleic acid (~75%) with a dry, fast-absorbing skin feel and mild astringent properties. It has very low comedogenic potential, is well-tolerated by sensitive, oily, and combination skin types, and absorbs readily without the greasy residue of heavier oils.

Coumarin is a fragrant lactone compound found naturally in tonka beans, lavender, and many other plants, classified as one of 26 known fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly in leave-on products with repeated exposure.

Cucumis sativus (cucumber) fruit extract is a soothing botanical rich in cucurbitacins, caffeic acid, and vitamin C with mild anti-inflammatory and skin-cooling properties. It is well-tolerated and used in calming and hydrating formulas for its ability to reduce redness and provide a refreshing, soothing sensation on reactive or puffy skin.

Curcuma longa (turmeric) root extract is rich in curcumin and related curcuminoids with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties documented across numerous studies. It is well-tolerated in formulas at typical concentrations, though high-curcumin extracts can temporarily stain the skin yellow and curcumin can occasionally cause contact sensitization in individuals with turmeric sensitivity.

Cyclohexane appearing in a cosmetic ingredient list typically indicates a trace residual from a solvent-extraction process rather than an intentional ingredient — it is an industrial aliphatic solvent regulated to minimal residual levels in finished cosmetic products. At trace concentrations it is considered low risk, but its presence reflects a manufacturing artifact rather than a functional skincare ingredient.

Cyclohexasiloxane (D6) is a cyclic volatile silicone used as a lightweight carrier and sensory modifier that evaporates after application, leaving behind actives and conditioning agents. While well-tolerated on skin, it has been flagged by the EU for environmental persistence and bioaccumulation in aquatic ecosystems — its use in rinse-off products is restricted in the EU, and it is increasingly being replaced in clean formulas.

Cyclomethicone is a blend of cyclic volatile silicones (typically D4 and D5) used as a lightweight carrier and spreading agent that evaporates after application without leaving a heavy residue. While skin-compatible, cyclic silicones including D4 and D5 have been restricted by the EU for use in rinse-off products due to environmental persistence and bioaccumulation concerns, and are increasingly replaced in clean formulas.

Cyclopentasiloxane (D5) is a volatile cyclic silicone used as a lightweight carrier and spreading agent that evaporates after application, imparting a silky skin feel without lasting residue. It has been restricted by the EU for use in rinse-off cosmetics due to environmental persistence and potential endocrine-disrupting properties at high aquatic concentrations, and is being phased out in clean-formula positioning.

Cymbidium grandiflorum (orchid) flower extract is a luxury botanical antioxidant providing polyphenols and polysaccharides with free-radical-scavenging and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in high-end anti-aging formulas for its antioxidant protection and marketing association with rare botanicals.

Cynara scolymus (artichoke) leaf extract is rich in cynarin, chlorogenic acid, and luteolin with potent antioxidant and mild brightening properties, used to protect skin from oxidative stress and support a more even tone. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and tone-evening formulas for its polyphenol antioxidant content.

Daucus carota sativa (carrot) root extract is rich in beta-carotene, carotenoids, and vitamin E with antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and brightening formulas for its ability to neutralize free radicals and support skin radiance.

Decyl glucoside is a mild sugar-based surfactant derived from glucose and decanol (from coconut or corn), used in gentle cleansers and foaming formulas for effective yet skin-friendly cleansing. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin, biodegradable, and is one of the most widely used natural surfactants in sulfate-free and clean-formula cleansers.

Decyl isostearate is a lightweight synthetic ester emollient derived from decanol and isostearic acid, providing a smooth, non-greasy skin feel with excellent spreadability. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used in lightweight moisturizers and primers as a skin-feel enhancer.

Decylene glycol (1,2-decanediol) is a long-chain C10 diol used as a preservative booster and mild antimicrobial agent that inhibits bacteria and fungi at low concentrations. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, and is frequently used in preservative-sensitive formulas alongside other mild preservative boosters.

Dextrin is a water-soluble polysaccharide derived from starch hydrolysis, used in skincare as a thickener, binder, and film-former that helps give formulas their body and consistency. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated across skin types.

Dextrin palmitate is a starch-palmitic acid ester used as a thickener and oil-binding agent in powders and anhydrous formulas, contributing a smooth, soft texture and helping to absorb excess oil. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing across skin types.

Dibutyl adipate is a lightweight synthetic diester emollient derived from butanol and adipic acid, providing a smooth, non-greasy skin feel and good spreadability. It is well-tolerated and non-comedogenic, commonly used as a skin-feel enhancer in moisturizers and sun care formulas.

Dicaprylyl carbonate is a lightweight, dry-feel ester emollient derived from caprylic acid, used as a non-greasy alternative to silicones and mineral oil in modern skincare formulas. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated across skin types, and provides excellent spreadability with a clean, fast-absorbing finish.

Dicaprylyl ether is a lightweight ether emollient derived from caprylic acid with a distinctively dry, silky skin feel and rapid absorption. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated across skin types, and is used as a silicone alternative in clean and natural-positioned formulas for its dry finish and excellent skin feel.

This entry appears to be an incomplete or truncated ingredient name — 'Diethylhexyl 2' is not a recognized standalone INCI ingredient and was likely captured as a data artifact during ingredient list parsing. The full name is probably 'Diethylhexyl 2,6-naphthalate' or a similar ester, but cannot be fully characterized without the complete name.

Diglycerin (diglycerol) is a humectant derived from glycerin that draws moisture to the skin surface and helps retain it in the upper skin layers. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and is used alongside glycerin and other polyols in hydrating formulas for its moisture-binding properties with slightly less stickiness than glycerin alone.
